content: A hybrid planning and learning system can leverage a tiered 'agent-of-agents' memory architecture where curiosity-driven sub-agents actively explore unstructured gaps in the system's long-term memory, while the top-tier hybrid planner prioritizes which discovered memory updates to integrate for downstream task performance. This design reframes active memory improvement not as a system-wide process, but as a decentralized negotiation between specialized exploratory agents and a centralized planning supervisor.
